inputs.name: accessibilityAttributes
inputs.description:
Defines the additional accessibility attributes that will be applied to the component.
The following fields are supported:

- **ariaKeyShortcuts**: Indicated the availability of a keyboard shortcuts defined for the menu item.

- **role**: Defines the role of the menu item. If not set, menu item will have default role="menuitem".

inputs.name: accessibleName
inputs.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleRole
inputs.description:
Used to define the role of the list item.

*Note:** If not set, the role is automatically inherited from the parent `ui5-list` based on its `accessible-role` property
(e.g. `Menu` -> `MenuItem`, `Tree` -> `TreeItem`, `ListBox` -> `Option`).
An explicitly set `accessible-role` on the list item takes precedence over the inherited role.

inputs.name: additionalText
inputs.description:
Defines the `additionalText`, displayed in the end of the menu item.

*Note:** The additional text will not be displayed if there are items added in `items` slot or there are
components added to `endContent` slot.

The priority of what will be displayed at the end of the menu item is as follows:
sub-menu arrow (if there are items added in `items` slot) -> components added in `endContent` -> text set to `additionalText`.

inputs.name: checked
inputs.description:
Defines whether menu item is in checked state.

*Note:** checked state is only taken into account when menu item is added to menu item group
with `checkMode` other than `None`.

*Note:** A checked menu item has a checkmark displayed at its end.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description:
Defines whether menu item is in disabled state.

*Note:** A disabled menu item is noninteractive.

inputs.name: highlight
inputs.description:
Defines the highlight state of the list items.
Available options are: `"None"` (by default), `"Positive"`, `"Critical"`, `"Information"` and `"Negative"`.

inputs.name: icon
inputs.description:
Defines the icon to be displayed as graphical element within the component.
The SAP-icons font provides numerous options.

*Example:**

See all the available icons in the [Icon Explorer](https://sdk.openui5.org/test-resources/sap/m/demokit/iconExplorer/webapp/index.html).

inputs.name: loading
inputs.description:
Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the loading indicator will be displayed inside the corresponding menu popover.

*Note:** If set to `true` a busy indicator component will be displayed into the related one to the current menu item sub-menu popover.

inputs.name: loadingDelay
inputs.description: Defines the delay in milliseconds, after which the loading indicator will be displayed inside the corresponding menu popover.

inputs.name: navigated
inputs.description:
The navigated state of the list item.
If set to `true`, a navigation indicator is displayed at the end of the list item.

inputs.name: selected
inputs.description: Defines the selected state of the component.

inputs.name: showSelection
inputs.description:
When set, a second line appears below the menu item text showing the text
of the currently selected sub-item. Intended for use with a single-select
ui5-menu-item-group (check-mode="Single").
When enabled, the checked sub-item cannot be unchecked,
ensuring the selection text is always displayed.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: Defines the text of the tree item.

inputs.name: tooltip
inputs.description: Defines the text of the tooltip for the menu item.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description:
Defines the visual indication and behavior of the list items.
Available options are `Active` (by default), `Inactive`, `Detail` and `Navigation`.

*Note:** When set to `Active` or `Navigation`, the item will provide visual response upon press and hover,
while with type `Inactive` and `Detail` - will not.

outputs.name: ui5BeforeClose
outputs.description: Fired before the menu is closed. This event can be cancelled, which will prevent the menu from closing.

outputs.name: ui5BeforeOpen
outputs.description:
Fired before the menu is opened. This event can be cancelled, which will prevent the menu from opening.

*Note:** Since 1.14.0 the event is also fired before a sub-menu opens.

outputs.name: ui5Check
outputs.description: Fired when an item is checked or unchecked.

outputs.name: ui5Click
outputs.description:
Fired when the component is activated either with a mouse/tap or by using the Enter or Space key.

*Note:** The event will not be fired if the `disabled` property is set to `true`.

outputs.name: ui5Close
outputs.description: Fired after the menu is closed.

outputs.name: ui5DetailClick
outputs.description: Fired when the user clicks on the detail button when type is `Detail`.

outputs.name: ui5Open
outputs.description: Fired after the menu is opened.
